Summary: How To Pack A Revolution In Your Suitcase is about artists in 1920s Germany who made new kinds of music, writing, and art. When the Nazis came to power in 1933, they tried to destroy these artists and their work. Many artists were hurt or jailed, but some got away and took their ideas with them around the world. Their art helped change the world. Bremner tells their stories and sings songs from that time, showing how their problems are like the ones we face today.

ART WINS AGAINST EVIL!!! In 1920’s Berlin, artists made crazy, beautiful art. Hitler tried to destroy them, but some escaped and kept fighting, kept making art, and CHANGED the world! 6506.bio:
$12.00 inc $2.50 fee 60 minutes 05: Acacia Hall
Show Contains: Adult Language/Content, Drugs/Alcohol, Mental Illness/Disorders, Religious Content, Sexual Content, Suicide

Summary: Multiple Neurosis is a storytelling show about a disease I have called Multiple Sclerosis. I tell stories about having a disability that is invisible. For example, when I was blind in one eye, other people did not notice. I talk about hiding my disability for 17 years, when going to parties, going on dates, and going to work. The show is about me trying to feel ok about having a disability, and not being ashamed. I have had Multiple Sclerosis for 20 years, and I finally tell people about it, instead of hiding it. I balance the serious parts of the show with fun and comedy.

Karin Fekko weaves tales of hilarity and tragedy through 17 years of keeping her MS a secret while partying, dating, and trying to land her dream job. This show is about invisible disability, internalized ableism, and big-time denial. Can we laugh about it? Karin thinks so. Join her as she marks 20 years since her MS diagnosis.

Summary: Bomb is a dark comedy about Dasha, a Ukrainian activist whose brain won’t shut up. The year is 2017. Dasha is trying to save the world, fix her relationship, and get her therapist to stop asking questions she doesn’t want to answer. It’s not going well. Her thoughts are spiraling, her reality is bending, and every conversation feels like a small explosion. As the pressure builds, Dasha starts to unravel... Bomb is fast, funny, darkly absurd, and a little bit tragic. It’s about burnout, big emotions, and staying sane when the world feels like it’s blowing up.

Dora Award winning director Lianna Makuch helms the Canadian premiere of Bomb by Ukrainian playwright Natalia Blok, featuring a star-studded cast (Rachel Bowron, Brett Dahl, Mariya Khomutova, and James MacDonald). Explosively funny and razor sharp, Bomb is a dark absurdist comedy that detonates with wit and strikes deep.

On a mission to craft the perfect play, struggling playwright Adam switches roles with his character Eve in a hopeless attempt to break through writer’s block. Genesis is a playful existential dive into love, freedom, and the stories we choose to (or not to) live in.

His lesson plans put a special focus on the 1920s silent film actor who is recognized as the all-time greatest star of the art form.

Summary: Hearts Left Open is about two women who meet outside their kids’ hospital rooms. One is a mother. One is an aunt. Both children have cancer. For a year, they wait together in the same hallway—worrying, hoping, and trying to survive. The nurses, doctors, and partners try to help, but no one has the answers. This is a story about what happens when life stays hard and people still show up. It’s about fear, love, anger—and the friendship that can grow from grief.

Two strangers. One hospital hallway. Over twelve months of grief, laughter, and impossible choices, they find the kind of friendship that saves lives. Hearts Left Open is raw, intimate, and unflinching—a story about showing up when hope runs out. Bring tissues. Stay for the truth.

Summary: This is a funny magic show combined with personal storytelling. Rob Teszka is a magician who used to work at a university. He tells the story of how he started in high school and ended up researching the psychology of how magic works. Every story involves an interactive magic trick. Some example topics include his high-school science award, lab meetings at the pub, and an improvised lecture. During the show, Rob talks about mistakes he made and makes fun of university life. He also invites people in the audience to share some of their own failings. With his magic and stories, Rob encourages people to laugh at their mistakes, and transform failure into success.

6557.name: The List: A Traumady About Probiotic Masculinity
6557.bio:
$12.00 inc $2.50 fee 60 minutes 23: The Bliss Stage
Show Contains: Adult Language/Content, Death, Sexual Content, Sexual Violence

Summary: The List is a true story about how a boy named Keith grows into a man. As a lonely kid in a small town, Keith looks up to the wrong people and makes a list of what he thinks it means to be a man, but it’s all bad advice. In university, he meets Jenny, who likes him even though he’s unsure of himself. Scared of getting close, Keith tells her a lie outside a KFC. That lie forces him to rethink everything, including his role models, and helps him see the good influences that shaped who he really is.

NOMINATED BEST STORYTELLING + BEST SCRIPT Montreal Fringe '24 Keith Serry's (PBS Stories From the Stage) hilarious and heartbreaking road trip from youth to old manhood is a search for guidance in a world where being “a good man” and being “the man” are two very different things. "Delightful...moving." - Forget the Box MTL

Playwright: Keith Serry
Director: David Crabb
Cast: Keith Serry

Summary: EXPOSED is a raw and honest storytelling show featuring true personal stories told in different dramatic styles. Created by Josie Nixon, it focuses on empathy, community, and healing through shared experiences. Each night opens with rotating stories, followed by the main act, Paducah Cheerleader, a powerful memoir about interracial dating in rural Kentucky. Audiences can return to hear new stories throughout the festival and even take part by submitting anonymous secrets, which are read aloud by JUICE the Trauma Clown to help build connection and understanding.

34X Award-Winning Producing Fringe Artist Beth Marshall is back headlining her own story, Paducah Cheerleader, about growing up in the Deep South of Ky. Hosted by JUICE The Trauma Clown. Raw & Real Stories.

Summary: The Witch of Edmonton is a modern retelling of a 1600s play about three people: Frank, forced into marriage; Elizabeth, accused of witchcraft; and Winnifred, caught in the chaos. It’s a story about choices, blame, and those who suffer because of them.

Accused, outcast, and condemned. The Witch of Edmonton is a gripping 1600s drama about fear, injustice, and the scapegoating of women, based on the real trial of Elizabeth Sawyer. For Fringe 2025, a revival in modern Edmonton—Set under the backdrop of a Jacobean Whyte Ave, Old Strathcona arch, and Tom, a devilish coyote puppet.

There is much that is funny / about a duck-billed platypus / but when they see a human / do they also laugh at us? | Adults deserve whimsy too! With poetry, interrobangs, and neurodivergence, Wade delights in exploring absurdity and making meaning in life. (Prev. Fringe: Charade, Most Honest Man. Jubilations: Howard in Big Bang)

Sept 1, 2022. A 24th birthday, a new home, a new school year, and a new name. And that's just the first day! This autobiographical one-person show tracks the following year: one filled with pain, stress, health problems, support, community, art, and love.

Summary: ZOMBIES, INC. is a one-person musical about Ray, who works in a New York jewelry store. When a zombie apocalypse begins, he gets stuck at work but still dreams about his future. Through songs and funny stories, Ray wonders if he was already living like a zombie before everything changed. The show is about love, loneliness, and what it means to be alive.

Zombies roam Manhattan. Ray’s still chasing commissions. Trapped inside New York’s iconic Rockefeller Plaza, one desperate jeweler battles the undead, late capitalism, and his unraveling dreams. A darkly funny solo musical with a killer score, ZOMBIES, INC. asks: what’s worth dying for—and what’s worth waking up to?

He learns from these sometimes humorous, sometimes tragic experiences and finds a way to free himself... or does he?

Galerie CAVA / 45 to 60 min / French and English performances / When all your deranged, sometimes humorous, sometimes tragic attempts at validation fail, what are you left with? Yourself? Inspired by her travels back to Ukraine, a young woman explores the impact of digging up your roots over shots of hooch. Its a Ukrainian kitchen-party with a live-band, if a Ukrainian kitchen party was held in a fried chicken restaurant.. Pull up a chair, grab a tasty bite, and raise your glass in a celebration of life. BUD’MO!

Sold out worldwide, this multi-award winning show ”will have you in stitches, whether you’re German or not.” (CBC, Canada). Experience hysterical dilemmas on the Autobahn, learn the truth about lederhosen, find out how the "no sense of humor" stereotype is both true and not true at all, and come to see: we Germans are not blunt or rude, we just value honesty more highly than other people’s feelings.

Wanna be German? Of course you do. And now you can, as German comedian Paco shows you how. Hard-hitting and hilarious, this is the first comic to hold a mirror up to Germany, Canada, and the world. This one-woman show follows a funny but struggling narrator as she deals with racism, identity, trauma, and being a teenager in the 1980s. Part stand-up, part theatre, it’s a personal and honest story about fighting to be yourself in a world that wants you to fit in. It’s about how we all carry pain, how we’re more alike than we think, and how being a bit messed up might be what makes us human.

Bollywood meets Breaking Bad? What if Saddam Hussein had a love child with Britney Spears? Summary: Kiki Moritsugu has a solo show where she talks about her strong connection with her mum, Jo Hutchings, who was a performer. In the show, Kiki sings, dances, and tells stories. She explains how growing up with a mum who was so bold and different helped her figure out who she is. Kiki is mixed race (part Japanese-Canadian and part English-Canadian), and she shares behind-the-scenes moments from her own job. She talks about how hard it was to find her own voice when other people had ideas about who she should be. The songs in the show are like a soundtrack to Kiki's life, helping us understand how she learned about herself. Now that Kiki has two kids who are also performers, the show ends with her feeling sure of herself and ready to make the future she wants for them.

Kiki Moritsugu holds forth on her relationship with her larger-than-life mother. Sharing three generations of backstage stories from mall shows in Canada to Broadway openings, Kiki reveals how one multi-racial woman defines herself in a world dominated by stereotypes. A Woman Is... a cabaret about becoming who you are.

Summary: Do you possess hidden psychic powers waiting to be unveiled? Prepare to discover your inner mystic—direct from London England, The Singing Psychic promises an evening brimming with glittering 70s-style games, toe-tapping tunes and prizes. The Singing Psychic Game Show has captivated audiences worldwide with its blend of comedy and uncanny accuracy. From revealing the melodies in your heart to dispensing relationship advice, probing life's deepest questions and inspiring audiences, The Singing Psychic leaves no stone unturned in her quest to entertain and enlighten. The biggest attraction is her spookily accurate (if utterly bonkers) audience readings. Theatregoers can expect a whirlwind of entertainment with a line-up of fabulous team games including psychic Jenga, psychic bingo, audience readings, freeze dance, for‘tunes’ and more. Each show is a unique experience fuelled by audience interaction and improvised, heart-warming hilarity. Whether arriving solo or with friends, there's a place for everyone in The Singing Psychic's realm of fun and mystery. As The Singing Psychic says “I have just two rules – one is that what I reveal in the room stays in the room, and the other is that I am ALWAYS right.” The Singing Psychic Game Show is brilliantly bananas. It’s an hour of fun that builds on three years of sell-out Edinburgh and Adelaide Fringe shows and four and five star Edinburgh Fringe reviews; a Funny Women Best Show nomination, two Montreal Fringe nominations (Prix de Creativite, Outstanding Comedy Show); a European tour; and a special appearance at the official BRIT Awards after- party. The Singing Psychic is also a TEDx speaker.

Spookily accurate (if bonkers) audience readings, songs & 70s team games. The Singing Psychic reveals the songs in your heart. Edinburgh & Adelaide Fringe sell-out show from the UK. Buttons & Pockets is a fable about creativity, the cosmos, & how we weave science and art into the fabric of reality. For kids, physicists, & the young at heart.

Five teenagers go on a weekend trip to a vacation property, only to find themselves face to face with a masked murderer who begins to kill each of them one by one. After they witness their friends being murdered, the remaining few start to ask themselves important questions, such as "Why didn't high school prepare me to defend myself from a murderer?" and "Who could want to do this to me?" even if from an audience perspective, it might seem obvious.

Ugh, teens are so dramatic. Always screaming when they get stabbed, and crying and flailing around when they get choked. Weekend trip? Next time, I think I'll skip. It's Scream meets Mean Girls, and this musical, from the creators of Krampus and Cult Cycle, is one to die for... The real question is "Who will be the Final Girl?"

In this modern cabaret show, she shares stories from the past ten years of her life—the happy times and the hard ones—and how music helped her get through it all. This emotional show is full of beautiful slow songs and fun, fast ones. She’s joined by Steven Greenfield on piano. He’s the music director behind big shows like Frozen and A Christmas Carol at the Citadel Theatre. Life can surprise us. One moment brings joy, the next brings heartbreak. We don’t get to choose which comes first.

Elena Porter, one of Edmonton Musical Theatre’s most evocative voices, shares the joy and pain of the past decade in a heartfelt cabaret. Accompanied by her friend, the incomparable Steven Greenfield on piano, she shows how music helped her survive when life came at her with two closed hands—one holding joy, the other heartbreak

6697.name: SHIRLEY GNOME: Children, You Can't Beat 'Em! (this is not a kid's show)
6697.bio:
$20.00 inc $5.00 fee 60 minutes 17: The Grindstone - Comedy Theatre & Bistro
Show Contains: Adult Language/Content, Cartoonish Violence, Death, Drugs/Alcohol, Mental Illness/Disorders, Political Content, Religious Content, Sexual Content, Smoke/Fog, Suicide

Summary: Shirley Gnome is a singer who has spent sixteen years performing songs about adult topics. Even though she’s been doing this for a long time, she’s never had a big hit. That changes when a video of her at a music festival, looking a bit "out of it", gets posted on TikTok by a teenager. The video quickly goes viral. A popular TikTok creator named DJ BBN0$e turns the video into a song that becomes a huge hit with kids — like the next “Baby Shark.” Suddenly, Shirley finds herself famous with a new, younger audience. She decides to go along with it to make more money. She’s invited to perform at an elite private school, hoping this new direction will finally pay off. But things quickly fall apart. The kids hate the songs, and her lyrics raise disturbing questions that no child (or parent) is ready for. Through this experience, Shirley starts to reflect on her fears about the world, the future, and the kind of planet we’re leaving behind. What starts as a cash grab gig turns into something deeper — a search for hope. Let’s be clear: this is not a real kids' show. It’s satire. Please do not bring your children. Seriously.

Juno nominee and last year’s holdover Shirley Gnome returns with a new direction. After accidentally creating a viral kids’ song on TikTok, the R-rated singer tries to cash in with a children’s music hour. Spoiler: it doesn’t go well. ★★★★★ “a bona fide genius” -Edmonton Journal. NOT SUITABLE FOR KIDS.

Playwright: Shirley Gnome, Becky Johnson
Cast: Shirley Gnome, children who wish to remain anonymous
